what_it_does: This file is an interpretive ledger entry for the source file app/canvases/StubCanvases.jsx. It records metadata (project, path, model) and a structured reading of the source: what the source does, observations about its contents, notable standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose-vs-actual assessment, themes, intention signals, novelty, contribution, and dense embedding summaries. It also declares that the source file contains no extractable code symbols.
observations: The file is a JSON interpretive layer keyed by project 'claude-ds' and source path 'app/canvases/StubCanvases.jsx'.
It includes a 'what_it_does' field summarizing the source as defining STUB_DEFS for six not-yet-built canvases and a StubCanvas React component.
The six stub canvas ids are type, logos, imagery, components, templates, and voice.
Each stub definition is described as carrying title, subtitle, description, and a four-item feature list.
The StubCanvas component is described as rendering a placeholder view with header, explanatory paragraph, feature bullets, and two buttons.
The interpretive layer notes that CanvasHeader and ViShape are referenced in the source but not imported in that file.
It records that the source attaches StubCanvas to window rather than exporting it as a module.
It includes evaluative/reflective fields such as purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, and contribution.
The symbols array is explicitly empty because the source contains no code symbols.
standouts: The interpretive layer captures both factual structure and inferred design intent for an unfinished design-system surface.
It documents the 'Ask Vi to build this canvas' button as a generative construction entry point.
It embeds brand-voice rules concretely inside the voice canvas definition.
conventions: Follows the ledger schema with consistent top-level metadata and a fixed set of interpretive fields.
Uses arrays of short strings for observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, questions, and themes.
Keeps embedding-oriented summary fields separate from free-form notes.
concerns: The interpretive file itself cannot verify claims about CanvasHeader and ViShape because it does not include the source code or external context.
It notes an inline style object in the source as a deviation from class-based styling discipline, but this is a second-hand observation rather than a verified fact in this file.
notes: A structured interpretive reading of a React stub-canvas file, intended to capture what the source does, how it is built, and what it seems to be planning for six future design-system canvases.
questions: What is the exact generation pipeline that produced this interpretive layer?
Where in the source or build system are CanvasHeader and ViShape actually defined?
Which canvases are considered the 'fully built' reference surfaces (Icons and Colors) and where are their interpretive entries?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file's purpose is to serve as an interpretive ledger entry for a source file, and that is exactly what it contains.
apparent_themes: Interpretive documentation as a first-class artifact in the codebase ledger.
Scaffolding-as-spec: placeholders that encode future capability.
Vi/AI generation as the intended path from stub to implemented surface.
Unified design-system surface treating every asset class as a parallel canvas.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to preserve the design intent and build brief for six unimplemented canvases so that future work — possibly AI-assisted — can continue from a coherent, documented starting point.
novelty: It treats a stub placeholder not as dead code but as an actionable, spec-carrying entry point for generative construction, and it encodes that reading in a structured ledger format.
contribution: Contributes a structured interpretive record of the StubCanvases.jsx source, including the enumerated canvas definitions, intended feature sets, component behavior notes, and inferred themes, suitable for fusion and downstream querying.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON interpretive ledger entry for the React source file StubCanvases.jsx, describing six placeholder design-system canvases, their feature specs, a reusable id-driven placeholder renderer, and the design intent behind them.
intention_for_embedding: To capture and preserve the purpose, structure, and future-build intent of the StubCanvases.jsx source file for retrieval and fusion with other ledger entries.
